description_long: This stone pagoda features a multi-tiered, square-based structure with a series of receding, stepped roofs leading up to a tall, slender spire. The base is broad and solid, supporting several stacked platforms, each with pronounced horizontal ledges and ornamental details. The central shaft rises vertically from the uppermost tier, culminating in a pointed finial that adds to the monument's vertical emphasis. The surfaces are weathered, with visible textural details suggesting carved stonework and architectural embellishments typical of traditional Buddhist monuments. The overall form is symmetrical, with each side displaying similar stepped elements and decorative motifs. The structure sits on a slightly raised platform, further enhancing its prominence and stability.
description: A multi-tiered stone pagoda with stepped roofs, ornate ledges, and a tall spire, featuring detailed stonework and a symmetrical, monumental form.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance-like recess and the layered, stepped roofs leading up to the central spire.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the stepped tiers and the solid, square base supporting the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of the roof tiers and the vertical shaft rising from the center.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the three-dimensional layering of the roofs and the pronounced finial at the top.
